title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
summary: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.
writeup: This short clip covers three practical workflow tips for using an AI coding assistant: running a security review to catch exposed API keys before deploying (a real failure mode that has produced five-figure cloud bills), using the clear command to end a task session and reclaim token budget, and creating reusable named skills by pasting a prompt so it can be invoked on demand. The through-line is deliberate session hygiene rather than letting the assistant accumulate context indefinitely.

similar.title: Claude API Sticker Shock: $100 a Week #minimax
Privacy Is the Real AI Bottleneck
Local AI Is Free—But There’s a Catch (Hybrid Setup Fix)
How Alex Hormozi ACTUALLY Uses AI

key_points: Skipping a security review before deploying vibe-coded apps is a documented failure mode: exposed API keys have resulted in bills as high as $20,000 when discovered and exploited by others.
The clear command ends the current conversation and starts a fresh session, preventing accumulated context from consuming the token budget across unrelated tasks.

Skills are saved, named prompt templates: pasting a prompt into a create-skill command stores it under a label (such as YouTube script polish) and makes it reusable in any future session without re-entering the full prompt.

chapters.title: Security Review and API Key Risk
